calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nelson Goodell appeared by telephone for Gregg Teets, Jan Moody and David Teets.
Stephen Webber in Propria Persona.

The Court raised the issue of the paralegal fees, questions whether the paralegal meets the required qualifications, whether the paralegal was the Decedent's agent and stated that the paralegal is charging more than what the Attorney is charging. Mr. Webber spoke of Ms. Woodall's various degrees and licenses, stated that she has no specific certifications, that he has Ms. Goodall's work product but, he did not file it because it is confidential, and that Ms. Woodall created a spread sheet of the bank accounts. Mr. Webber also stated that the management company deposited funds in the amount of $4,000.00 a month from the real estate into the bank account, that numerous payments were made from the bank account for the mortgage and the home equity loan, and that transfers were made to a savings account for extraordinary expenses, repairs, countertops, etc. In addition, Mr. Webber stated that after Mr. Teets date of death there was a balance in the account that belonged to the Spouse, that it was left there, that the spread sheet was extremely helpful to him in breaking down the expenses, that $350.00 an hour to Ms. Woodall is extremely reasonable because the estate was extremely complicated, and that the taxes went back three years and had to be split between the Decedent's estate and the Spouse. Mr. Goodell stated that Mr. Webber did not serve him with the April 4th or the June 25th supplements, that he objects to the supplements, that Mr. Webber has refused to e-mail him and that he sent Mr. Webber an e-mail yesterday about today's hearing and he got no response from Mr. Webber. Mr. Goodell also stated that paralegals do not do taxes, spoke of the qualifications of doing taxes, stated that Ms. Woodall's rate is more than his fees, that the rate is unconscionable and that the law does not support the paralegal fees. In addition, Mr. Webber requested that the Court not grant the paralegal's fees, and stated that he has read the June supplement, but he had no chance to analyze it, The Court noted that it has not looked at the documents that were brought in by Mr. Webber, or received them. Mr. Webber stated that he has detailed why the real estate took so long. Mr. Goodell stated that there was an eight or nine month period where nothing was done, that his Clients requested that the Administrator sell the property, that the fees for sale of the house are grossly unjustified, that the Decedent's Wife should get none of the loan pay back and that Mr. Webber's fees should be as a regular person, not as an Attorney. The Amended First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2903 Harrison St., San Francisco, Ca 94110)
Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Webber in Propria Persona.
Nelson Goodell for Heirs.

The Buyer was present and waives notice. 100% of the property is being sold. The estate holds a 37.195% interest in the property. The sale was confirmed at $1,320,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $417,000.00 is required.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nelson Goodell for Gregg Teets, Janet Teets Moody and David Teets (Heirs).
Stephen Webber (Petitioner/Administrator) in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Mr. Webber that the request to enter into a contract for exclusive right to sell real property is a relief that can not be granted by way of a supplement and that he can file an ex parte petition. Mr. Webber stated that he wants to sell the property as soon as possible, that it will take a year to wrap up the Estate, that he has no idea how long it will take to sell the property and that the only asset is the property. The Court questions why it will take that long to close out the Estate and stated that it will continue administration of the Estate for six months. Mr. Goodell stated that he agrees with selling the property, that one year for distribution of the Estate is a long time and that his Clients have been waiting a long time. The Court granted the petition for approval of report on status of administration excluding the request for exclusive right to sell (subject to review of the order). Status on close of estate is set for January 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Continued To Jun-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sandra Price and Deanna Leonida for Ekaterina Teets (Petitioner)
Stephen Webber in propria persona (Administrator)

The Court noted there are no objections. Ms. Price stated she would like to submit a revised order, the numbers in the petition (29% / 71%) are not correct, the surviving spouse is aware of the 4% difference, and the forensic accounting (25% / 75%) is correct. Mr. Webber stated that he spoke to the three siblings, and this calculation benefits them. The Court reviewed the (unfiled) revised petition and emails to/from the Petitioner, provided by Ms. Price, and instructed Ms. Price to file the amendment to petition with a verification by the Petitioner. The Spousal Property Petition is continued to June 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-07-2018 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights, For An Order Confirming Surviving Spouses Ownership Of Real Property, For An Order That Estate Not Be Administered, And An Order Waiving Accounting And Administrators Fees
Off Calendar Jun-16-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen E. Webber, petitioner in pro per.

Mr. Webber informed that Court that he had known the decedent very well for many years (they had been law school roommates), and that throughout the 13 years of his marriage to his surviving spouse he alwats referred to the property in question as their property and not his, and that such supported the contention that he had transmuted the property into community property with joint ownership. He added that he had spoken with each of the decedent's three siblings, and that none had objected to the petition. In response to the Court's inquiry as to the effect of denial of the petition, he responded that the numerous loans and other transactions made it extremely difficult to determine if there would be any change in rights, as it would depend upon the ultimate value of the property and tracing of loan payments and transactions over many years. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)
